growth_records.agit: 200 rpm (shaking incubator) for planktonic growth assays; static in anaerobic chamber for co-culture/biofilm
growth_records.cite: Cao et al. The interactive relationship between Fusobacterium necrophorum, Bacteroides pyogenes, and Porphyromonas levii in driving inflammatory uterine disease. Microbiome (2026). DOI:10.1186/s40168-025-02320-6. [PMC12911390]
growth_records.snippet: F. necrophorum grew rapidly, exhibiting the highest maximum specific growth rate (µmax = 0.593 h−1) and the shortest doubling time (1.17 h), and entered the stationary phase after 6 h of incubation.
growth_records.notes: Both mu and doubling time directly reported in text (Results, planktonic growth section); internally consistent with mu=ln2/DT (ln2/0.593h-1=1.169h ~1.17h). Culture: Hungate anaerobic tubes, 200 rpm shaking, 37C, anaerobic chamber (5% H2,5% CO2,90% N2).
